openmeter/productcatalog/plan/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing plan.Repository for plan lifecycle persistence (CRUD, phase management, rate card bulk creation). Primary constraint: every method body must wrap Ent calls in entutils.TransactingRepo to honor ctx-carried transactions.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping on every method
patterns.description: Every exported adapter method body must be a closure passed to entutils.TransactingRepo[T, *adapter](ctx, a, fn) so the call rebinds to any transaction already in ctx. Failure to do so silently reads/writes outside the caller's transaction.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo[*plan.Plan, *adapter](ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, a *adapter) (*plan.Plan, error) { ... })

patterns.name: Tx / WithTx / Self triad
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx (HijackTx + NewTxDriver), WithTx (NewTxClientFromRawConfig returning a new *adapter), and Self() returning itself. All three must be present or TransactingRepo panics.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txClient := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txClient.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Config.Validate() on construction
patterns.description: New(Config) calls config.Validate() which checks that Client and Logger are non-nil; returns error on failure. Config satisfies var _ models.Validator = (*Config)(nil).
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (plan.Repository, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Typed eager-load var helpers
patterns.description: Reusable query modifier vars (planPhaseEagerLoadRateCardsFn, rateCardEagerLoadFeaturesFn, rateCardEagerLoadTaxCodesFn, planEagerLoadActiveAddons) compose WithRatecards/WithFeatures/WithTaxCode so every query loads the full graph consistently.

patterns.name: Soft-delete via DeletedAt, phases NOT soft-deleted
patterns.description: DeletePlan sets DeletedAt on the plan row only. Phases are hard-deleted on DeletePlan and on UpdatePlan phase reconciliation so pre-deletion state remains inspectable via the plan row. Queries filter with plandb.DeletedAtIsNil() unless IncludeDeleted is true.

patterns.name: Status filtering via clock.Now() predicates
patterns.description: ListPlans maps PlanStatus enum values (Draft, Active, Archived, Scheduled, Invalid) to EffectiveFrom/EffectiveTo Ent predicates using clock.Now().UTC() so tests can override time via clock.SetTime.
patterns.example: now := clock.Now().UTC(); predicates = append(predicates, plandb.And(plandb.EffectiveFromLTE(now), plandb.Or(plandb.EffectiveToGTE(now), plandb.EffectiveToIsNil())))

patterns.name: Bulk rate card create then re-query
patterns.description: After CreateBulk for rate cards, the phase row is re-fetched with WithRatecards+WithFeatures+WithTaxCode eager loads to return a fully populated struct instead of relying on in-memory assembly.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, constructor New(Config), and the Tx/WithTx/Self transaction rebinding plumbing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never store a tx-scoped client on the struct permanently; WithTx must return a new *adapter with the tx-bound db.

key_file_guides.role: All core plan CRUD (ListPlans, CreatePlan, GetPlan, UpdatePlan, DeletePlan). UpdatePlan phase reconciliation uses delete-all + re-create via createPhase, not an incremental diff.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Callers of UpdatePlan must supply the FULL phase set when Phases != nil; nil Phases skips phase reconciliation entirely.

key_file_guides.role: createPhase helper and rateCardBulkCreate — internal helpers called by CreatePlan and UpdatePlan. Both accept a raw *entdb.PlanRateCardClient so they must always be called from within a TransactingRepo closure.
key_file_guides.watch_for: rateCardBulkCreate builds []*entdb.PlanRateCardCreate but does NOT execute; the caller must call CreateBulk(...).Exec(ctx).

key_file_guides.role: Bidirectional Ent↔domain conversion: FromPlanRow, fromPlanPhaseRow, fromPlanRateCardRow, asPlanRateCardRow. Uses p.Edges.AddonsOrErr() to distinguish 'not loaded' (nil) from 'loaded empty slice'.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New plan fields added to the Ent schema must be mapped in both directions; a missing field silently drops data. TaxConfig must call productcatalog.BackfillTaxConfig after mapping TaxCode edge.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db directly outside a TransactingRepo closure — bypasses ctx-carried transaction.
Assembling plan.Plan from in-memory state after CreateBulk instead of re-querying with eager loads.
Returning plain entdb.IsNotFound(err) without wrapping in plan.NewNotFoundError — callers expect domain errors.
Soft-deleting phases on plan deletion — breaks ability to inspect pre-deletion plan state.
Directly editing openmeter/ent/db/ generated files — always regenerate with make generate.

decisions.decision: All methods wrap Ent access in entutils.TransactingRepo even for reads.
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate via ctx; not rebinding causes a helper to fall off the caller's transaction, risking partial writes during multi-step operations.

decisions.decision: Phases are deleted and re-created wholesale on UpdatePlan when params.Phases != nil.
decisions.rationale: Simplifies correctness over incremental diffing; callers that only change plan metadata pass nil Phases to skip phase reconciliation.

decisions.decision: Plan status is derived from EffectiveFrom/EffectiveTo at query time using clock predicates, not stored in a separate status column.
decisions.rationale: Avoids dual-write consistency bugs; no separate status field means Atlas migrations are not needed when status logic changes.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new adapter method that queries and writes inside the caller's transaction

openmeter/productcatalog/plan/httpdriver.purpose: v1 HTTP handler layer for plan lifecycle endpoints (List, Create, Update, Delete, Get, Publish, Archive, Next), translating between api.* types and plan.Service calls via httptransport.Handler. Primary constraint: never call Ent or plan.Repository directly — all persistence goes through plan.Service.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs with typed aliases
patterns.description: Each endpoint declares three type aliases (XxxRequest, XxxResponse, XxxHandler) then returns a handler built with decoder, operation, encoder, and AppendOptions. This makes the interface method signature self-documenting.
patterns.example: type (ListPlansRequest = plan.ListPlansInput; ListPlansResponse = api.PlanPaginatedResponse; ListPlansHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[ListPlansRequest, ListPlansResponse, ListPlansParams])

patterns.name: Namespace decoded from context in every decoder
patterns.description: h.resolveNamespace(ctx) must be called in every request decoder closure. Failure returns commonhttp.NewHTTPError(500) because the namespace is always set by middleware upstream.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Req{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve namespace: %w", err) }

patterns.name: credits.Enabled guard in decoder for settlement mode
patterns.description: CreatePlan and UpdatePlan decoders reject CreditOnlySettlementMode when h.credits.Enabled is false, returning models.NewGenericValidationError. Guard belongs in the decoder, not in the operation or service.
patterns.example: if !h.credits.Enabled && req.SettlementMode == productcatalog.CreditOnlySettlementMode { return CreatePlanRequest{}, models.NewGenericValidationError(...) }

patterns.name: ValidationErrorEncoder on every handler
patterns.description: Every endpoint appends httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ValidationErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ResourceKindPlan)) so plan validation issues map to proper HTTP status codes.

patterns.name: ref.ParseIDOrKey for dual ID/key routing
patterns.description: GetPlan and NextPlan use ref.ParseIDOrKey to distinguish ULID IDs from string keys without separate endpoints.
patterns.example: idOrKey := ref.ParseIDOrKey(params.IDOrKey); return GetPlanRequest{NamespacedID: models.NamespacedID{Namespace: ns, ID: idOrKey.ID}, Key: idOrKey.Key}

key_file_guides.role: Declares Handler and PlanHandler interfaces, the handler struct, and New constructor. credits appconfig.CreditsConfiguration is injected here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: credits must be passed to New; omitting it silently allows CreditOnly plans when credits are disabled.

key_file_guides.role: All endpoint implementations. IgnoreNonCriticalIssues=true is set in Create and Update decoders; PublishPlan sets EffectiveFrom to clock.Now(); ArchivePlan sets EffectiveTo to clock.Now().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not remove IgnoreNonCriticalIssues=true — plans may have non-critical validation issues during create/update.

key_file_guides.role: FromPlan, FromPlanPhase, AsCreatePlanRequest, AsUpdatePlanRequest, AsPlanPhase — api↔domain conversion. Delegates rate card mapping to productcatalog/http.FromRateCard and AsRateCards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New plan fields must be mapped in both FromPlan and AsCreatePlanRequest/AsUpdatePlanRequest; missing fields are silently ignored at the API boundary.

anti_patterns: Importing entdb or calling plan.Repository directly — all access must go through plan.Service.
Hardcoding namespace strings instead of calling h.resolveNamespace(ctx).
Omitting httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(productcataloghttp.ValidationErrorEncoder(...)) — plan validation errors won't map to proper HTTP status codes.
Putting the credits.Enabled guard in the operation closure instead of the decoder — decode-phase guards run before any service call.
Setting EffectivePeriod directly in UpdatePlan decoder — status transitions must go through PublishPlan/ArchivePlan.

decisions.decision: Credits feature guard is duplicated in both CreatePlan and UpdatePlan decoders.
decisions.rationale: credits.enabled must be enforced at the HTTP boundary; the service layer does not know about deployment configuration.

decisions.decision: PublishPlan and ArchivePlan are separate explicit operations, not flags on UpdatePlan.
decisions.rationale: Prevents callers from manipulating plan status via UpdatePlan; EffectivePeriod is explicitly zeroed in UpdatePlan so status transitions are gated through explicit publish/archive paths.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new plan endpoint following the standard three-alias + handler pattern

openmeter/productcatalog/plan/service.purpose: Business-logic service implementing plan.Service: validates inputs, resolves feature/taxcode cross-references, enforces status-based mutation guards, delegates persistence to plan.Repository (adapter), and publishes domain events via Watermill. Primary constraint: all mutations must wrap adapter calls and event publishing inside transaction.Run.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wrapping for all mutations
patterns.description: All mutating methods (Create, Update, Delete, Publish, Archive, Next) wrap their adapter calls and publisher.Publish inside transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, fn) to ensure atomicity.

patterns.name: resolveFeatures + resolveTaxCodes before adapter write
patterns.description: Before CreatePlan/UpdatePlan hits the adapter, s.resolveFeatures populates FeatureKey↔FeatureID cross-references and s.resolveTaxCodes populates TaxCodeID. Missing features are converted to GenericValidationError.
patterns.example: if err = s.resolveFeatures(ctx, params.Namespace, &phase.RateCards); err != nil { if models.IsGenericNotFoundError(err) { err = models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }; return nil, err }

patterns.name: Event publishing inside transaction closure
patterns.description: After adapter mutation succeeds, each mutating method calls s.publisher.Publish(ctx, plan.NewPlanXxxEvent(ctx, p)) inside the transaction closure. If publish fails the transaction rolls back.
patterns.example: event := plan.NewPlanCreateEvent(ctx, p); if err := s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to publish plan created event: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Status guard before mutations
patterns.description: UpdatePlan, DeletePlan, PublishPlan, ArchivePlan each check allowed statuses via lo.Contains before delegating to adapter; violations return models.NewGenericValidationError.
patterns.example: allowedPlanStatuses := []productcatalog.PlanStatus{productcatalog.PlanStatusDraft, productcatalog.PlanStatusScheduled}; if !lo.Contains(allowedPlanStatuses, p.Status()) { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(...) }

patterns.name: Config-based constructor with non-nil dependency validation
patterns.description: New(Config) validates that Adapter, Feature, TaxCode, Logger, and Publisher are all non-nil before returning service. var _ plan.Service = (*service)(nil) ensures compile-time interface satisfaction.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (plan.Service, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return service{...}, nil }

patterns.name: Version auto-increment in CreatePlan
patterns.description: CreatePlan queries all existing versions for the plan key and auto-sets params.Version = max(existing) + 1. Callers must not pass a version; the service enforces monotonically increasing versions.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, New constructor, and service struct. All dependency fields (adapter, feature, taxCode, logger, publisher) are private.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new dependency requires updating Config validation, Config struct, and service struct — all three must stay in sync.

key_file_guides.role: All plan.Service method implementations plus resolveFeatures and resolveTaxCodes private helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EffectivePeriod is explicitly zeroed in UpdatePlan to prevent direct status manipulation; do not allow callers to set it via this method. Default settlement mode CreditThenInvoice is applied here when not provided.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests using pctestutils.NewTestEnv which wires the full stack (adapter + service). Tests drive behavior through env.Plan (service) not env.PlanRepository (adapter).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use context.Background(); in new tests prefer t.Context() per project convention.

anti_patterns: Calling s.adapter directly for mutations without wrapping in transaction.Run — breaks atomicity with event publishing.
Allowing EffectivePeriod to be set via UpdatePlan — it is explicitly zeroed to prevent direct status manipulation.
Skipping resolveFeatures before CreatePlan/UpdatePlan — rate cards with FeatureKey-only or FeatureID-only refs will have incomplete cross-references stored in the DB.
Publishing events outside the transaction closure — if DB write succeeds but publish fails the transaction only rolls back when both are inside transaction.Run.
Returning entdb or adapter-level errors directly to callers — convert them to domain errors (models.GenericNotFoundError, models.GenericValidationError) at the service boundary.

decisions.decision: Feature and TaxCode resolution is done in the service layer, not in the adapter.
decisions.rationale: Adapter handles only persistence; cross-entity lookups (feature by key/ID, tax code by stripe code) are orchestration concerns that belong in the service.

decisions.decision: Version is auto-incremented from existing versions; callers cannot set it directly.
decisions.rationale: Prevents version gaps and ensures monotonically increasing versions per plan key across concurrent create calls.

decisions.decision: Default settlement mode CreditThenInvoice is applied in the service, not in the HTTP handler.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the default a domain invariant independent of the transport layer so non-HTTP callers get the same default.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new mutating service method that calls the adapter and publishes an event
